item.title: New Driver for the Old Griffin PowerMate 摘要
item.description:
James Lockman has developed a modern driver for the Griffin PowerMate, a vintage USB knob controller originally designed for audio/video production. The device features a twistable knob, a press function, and a blue LED that changes intensity. Lockman credits Cursor Agent, an AI coding tool, as a co-contributor, suggesting this project was enabled by AI assistance. The driver revives a nostalgic piece of hardware that, while superseded by more advanced controllers, retains a quaint charm.
标签: Griffin PowerMate, driver, retro hardware, USB controller, AI coding, Cursor Agent, open source, nostalgia, hardware revival

item.title: Klack 摘要
item.description:
Klack is a $5 Mac utility by Henrik Ruscon that simulates mechanical keyboard clacking sounds while typing. Despite the author's own keyboard making natural sounds, he found the absurd utility appealing and attempted to purchase it, only to discover a family member had already bought it. A reader also shared buying it out of spite to troll a colleague using a mechanical keyboard in an open office.
标签: mac, utility, keyboard, sound, humor, app store, productivity, trolling

item.title: The Trump T1 Phone Starts Shipping This Week, Supposedly 摘要
item.description:
Trump Mobile's T1 phone, announced 11 months ago, is finally shipping to preorder customers this week, as confirmed by CEO Pat O'Brien to USA Today. The company cited incredibly high demand and is fulfilling orders as quickly as possible. The phone features 'Trump Mobile' branding in Arial font on the camera plateau.
标签: Trump Mobile, T1 Phone, smartphone, shipping, preorder, politics, tech, USA Today

item.title: A GNU Emacs learning experience with text-mode hooks 摘要
item.description:
The author describes debugging an issue where Emacs would complain about an ispell process still running when quitting. Initially suspecting code buffers triggering text-mode hooks, they discovered that Flycheck's scratch buffers derive from text-mode, causing flyspell-mode to start ispell. The solution was to guard the text-mode hook with a check for `buffer-file-name` to ensure it only runs in file-associated buffers, preventing flyspell from activating in scratch buffers.
标签: Emacs, flyspell, ispell, text-mode, Flycheck, debugging, ELisp, scratch buffers, buffer-file-name, hooks

item.title: Gurman Reports that OpenAI Is Unhappy With Apple Deal 摘要
item.description:
Bloomberg reports that OpenAI is considering legal action against Apple, alleging breach of contract over their partnership that integrated ChatGPT into Apple software. OpenAI claims Apple failed to deliver promised deeper integration and prime Siri placement, while Apple also expected more integration. OpenAI executives express frustration, stating Apple demanded a 'leap of faith' that didn't pay off. However, the article notes that ChatGPT remains highly popular on the App Store, and questions the timing of OpenAI's complaints, which emerged after Apple partnered with Google for foundation models. The specific contractual terms allegedly breached remain unclear.
标签: Apple, OpenAI, ChatGPT, legal dispute, partnership, AI, Bloomberg, contract breach, Siri, App Store

item.title: Google Announces Its Chromebook Successor: The Googlebook 摘要
item.description:
Google has announced Googlebooks, a new line of laptops launching in fall 2026, intended to succeed Chromebooks. They will run a fusion of Android and ChromeOS. Details are scarce: no specs, model names, or pricing yet. Google is partnering with Acer, Asus, Dell, HP, and Lenovo. A notable feature is 'Magic Pointer,' which provides contextual suggestions when shaking the cursor over on-screen items. The announcement was part of Google's Android Show, with only initial renders available. The Verge's Di Benedetto expresses skepticism due to the lack of concrete information.
标签: Google, Googlebook, laptop, Android, ChromeOS, Magic Pointer, hardware, announcement, Chromebook, operating system

item.title: Tim Cook Is in Trump’s Executive Entourage for China Summit 摘要
item.description:
Owen Scott reports that tech and finance leaders including Elon Musk, BlackRock CEO Larry Fink, and Apple CEO Tim Cook have joined President Trump for a summit with China's President Xi Jinping. Trump confirmed the attendees on Truth Social, referring to Cook as 'Tim Apple'. The article also notes Trump's nickname habit and suggests he could reference 'Winnie the Pooh' in his posts.
标签: politics, technology, China, Trump, Xi Jinping, Elon Musk, Tim Cook, summit, business, nicknames

item.title: Geoffrey Fowler and the Launch of the Youth AI Safety Institute 摘要
item.description:
Geoffrey Fowler joins the Youth AI Safety Institute, a new organization under Common Sense Media with a $20 million annual budget, to systematically test AI products used by children, set safety standards, and hold tech companies accountable. The Institute is funded by foundations and private donors including Anthropic and the OpenAI Foundation, who have no say over published findings. Fowler's role as Head of Public Engagement involves translating research into actionable insights for families, educators, and policymakers. The author expresses skepticism about industry-funded oversight, questioning the effectiveness of Biden's AI Executive Order.
标签: AI safety, children, Common Sense Media, Geoffrey Fowler, Youth AI Safety Institute, tech accountability, AI regulation, product testing, Anthropic, OpenAI

item.title: A few words on DS4 摘要
item.description:
Antirez reflects on the rapid popularity of DwarfStar 4 (DS4), attributing it to the release of DeepSeek v4 Flash, a quasi-frontier model that is large and fast enough for local inference, especially with 2/8 bit asymmetric quants on 96-128GB RAM. He worked 14-hour days for a week, leveraging GPT 5.5 and local AI experience. Future plans include supporting new open-weight models, specialized variants (coding, legal, medical), quality benchmarks, a coding agent, CI testing, more ports, and distributed inference. He notes this is the first time he uses a local model for serious tasks instead of Claude/GPT, calling it a big milestone.
标签: AI, local inference, DeepSeek, DwarfStar, open source, LLM, model quantization, distributed inference, coding agent, hardware

item.title: Quoting Mitchell Hashimoto 摘要
item.description:
Mitchell Hashimoto reflects on the fungibility of programming languages, noting that Bun's rewrite from Zig to Rust in roughly a week demonstrates how languages are no longer a lock-in. He argues that Rust, like any language, is expendable and can be replaced when no longer useful, highlighting a shift in the software industry where languages are increasingly interchangeable.
标签: programming languages, Rust, Zig, Bun, software engineering, rewrite, fungibility, lock-in

item.title: Meta’s New Reality: Record High Profits. Record Low Morale | WIRED 摘要
item.description:
A Wired report reveals historically low morale at Meta as employees brace for layoffs on May 20. Staff describe unhappiness, lack of mission connection, and fear of being used to train AI replacements. Many hope to be laid off for severance. New mandatory employee surveillance software (MCI) has sparked privacy concerns, with CTO Andrew Bosworth allegedly berating dissenters. Only top AI executives are thriving.
标签: Meta, layoffs, employee morale, surveillance, AI, privacy, tech industry, workplace culture, Wired

item.title: Not so locked in any more 摘要
item.description:
A medium-sized tech company used a coding agent to rewrite its legacy iPhone and Android apps into React Native, citing improved framework capabilities and the reduced cost of future re-rewrites. This reflects a broader trend where programming languages and frameworks are no longer permanent lock-in, as AI-assisted coding lowers the barrier to porting between technologies. The decision was made with the mindset that if React Native proves wrong, they can easily port back to native apps.
标签: react-native, coding-agents, ai-assisted-programming, generative-ai, ai, llms, react, mobile-development, software-engineering, tech-trends
